details_md:
1. files changed

[tests/ui-redesign.test.mjs](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/tests/ui-redesign.test.mjs)  
[tests/helpers/site-test-utils.mjs](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/tests/helpers/site-test-utils.mjs)

2. what was done

Added a real `node:test` suite for the redesign spec. The tests now follow the repo’s existing convention: build once, inspect source/CSS/built HTML, and assert the spec items by test ID `TS-01` through `TS-09`.

I also added small helper support for path existence and local port capability detection so the suite can validate build output reliably and skip the dev-preview check only when the environment blocks loopback binding.

3. why this is minimal

The change stays inside `tests/` and does not touch site code, content schema, or runtime behavior. It reuses the existing Node test harness and existing helper style instead of introducing a new test framework.

4. validation performed

Ran `npm test -- --test tests/ui-redesign.test.mjs`.

Current result:
- Pass: `TS-01`, `TS-08`
- Skip: `TS-09` in this sandbox because local port binding is blocked
- Fail as expected against current implementation: `TS-02`, `TS-03`, `TS-04`, `TS-05`, `TS-06`, `TS-07`

5. open risks

The suite is intentionally asserting the redesign target state, so several tests fail until the UI work is implemented.

`TS-09` depends on local dev server access. In this environment it is skipped because `127.0.0.1` binding returns `EPERM`; on a normal local machine it should run.

6. recommended next step

Implement the redesign changes in CSS/components/content, then rerun `npm test -- --test tests/ui-redesign.test.mjs` and `npm run build`.
